--- Thread (25 messages, 2019-10-17) ---
[2019-10-17 13:06] drab_emordnilap (Drab Emordnilap): What about 2v2, AB vs CD. two separate simultaneous games. A vs C and D vs B. Same set. Each team has one member going first and one second. When one player is eliminated, that team loses. (Draw if each team loses a player on the same turn number)
[2019-10-17 13:07] drab_emordnilap (Drab Emordnilap): So like, turn 1, A and D play their dd turn and their actions are revealed once both lock in. Then B and C play their p2t1. Etc etc
[2019-10-17 13:08] drevoed: What's the fun in that?
[2019-10-17 13:09] drevoed: All I see is two 1v1s, just with an incentive to drag out a game once you see you are losing.
[2019-10-17 13:10] drab_emordnilap (Drab Emordnilap): The “fun” is presumably that prismata is fun already
[2019-10-17 13:10] drab_emordnilap (Drab Emordnilap): The benefit to this format specifically is that there’s no seat advantage
[2019-10-17 13:10] drevoed: are they playing the same set?
[2019-10-17 13:12] drevoed: Or can we always force a draw by mirroring?
[2019-10-17 13:13] drab_emordnilap (Drab Emordnilap): Who are you mirroring? If you’re p1, you can’t mirror opponents team p1 because you play at the same time
[2019-10-17 13:14] drab_emordnilap (Drab Emordnilap): Re:comms, “no” means people discord chat anyways. Seems hard to enforce
[2019-10-17 13:14] drevoed: So we are always roping every turn now, to prevent mirrors?
[2019-10-17 13:14] drab_emordnilap (Drab Emordnilap): Turn isn’t revealed until both p1s lock in
[2019-10-17 13:14] awaclus: alternatively it could be a bullet game so you don't have the time to mirror
[2019-10-17 13:16] drevoed: Do we pause the game when waiting for the other game to finish the same turn?
[2019-10-17 13:18] drab_emordnilap (Drab Emordnilap): Like, timer? You’re p1, you spend 10 seconds of your 60 locking in your turn, you hit confirm, no one else has seen your actions yet until other team p1 also hits confirm. Then it’s both p2s turns and they see what you bought and incoming damage etc etc
[2019-10-17 13:19] drevoed: Who's timer is ticking when we are waiting for the other table?
[2019-10-17 13:19] drab_emordnilap (Drab Emordnilap): If game A p1 finished turn before game@B p1, there’s a time when in game A, no one’s clocks is running
[2019-10-17 13:19] drevoed: But we get the time to think about our moves.
[2019-10-17 13:19] drab_emordnilap (Drab Emordnilap): Can’t be p1, they confirmed their turn. Can’t be p2, they can’t act yet
[2019-10-17 13:20] drab_emordnilap (Drab Emordnilap): Sure, with incomplete info. But you can reduce turn timers in the game also if you want
[2019-10-17 13:20] drab_emordnilap (Drab Emordnilap): Maybe standard time controls are shorter in this mode
[2019-10-17 13:21] shadourow (Rando): I think it's ok.
Both playere have time to think in that case
[2019-10-17 13:22] drevoed: So I can trade my time bank for my buddy's.
[2019-10-17 13:22] drevoed: Or just wait 60 seconds every time just in case his opponent finishes his turn quickly.
[2019-10-17 13:23] drab_emordnilap (Drab Emordnilap): Heck, maybe you and your buddy share a time bank to begin with. 🕰 🏦

--- Thread (7 messages, 2019-10-17) ---
[2019-10-17 22:44] hyreon: okay, with or without simultaneous turns, both T1 and T2 get to attack the *entire team* when they attack. But rather than doing the sum total of both of their attacks, they come in waves -- p1 (or p2's), then p3 (or p4's).
[2019-10-17 22:44] hyreon: I feel like both players need to share a defense, because that's one of the key components from the swade / anya campaign levels and it worked very nicely.
[2019-10-17 22:46] hyreon: So if your ally (T1-1, P1) deals 12 damage and you (T1-2, P4) deal 4, the opponent has to defend the 12 damage and then the 4, and regardless of who assigns the defense, they can use both P2 and P3's defenders.
This lets you deny absorb, use defensive finesse and all that other good stuff that you normally can pull off.
[2019-10-17 22:48] hyreon: Now on to the breach UI. Any time the opponent *can* deal enough damage to break through your defenders, it is marked as yellow. Any time it *must*, where your highest absorber isn't good enough to shrug off the first attack, then it is marked as red.
[2019-10-17 22:49] Deleted User (Deleted User): i like the wave idea, dislike the shared defense idea, but it's not a strong islike and i could be persuadded
[2019-10-17 22:51] hyreon: And when the second wave arrives, if it deals enough damage to overrun defenders, *that's* when they get to decide where the attackers go.
[2019-10-17 22:51] hyreon: I'm not a huge fan of that, but as long as your units come in waves and all units can be targetted by all players, then there's not much way around it.

[2019-10-17 23:55] hyreon: Catan Order 4-player. A ruleset for 4 player games where p1 goes, then p2 and p3 (who are both on a team), then p4 and p1 (who are both on a team).
[2019-10-17 23:56] jeacaveo (jeacaveo (#NeverBrowserer)): Ooooh, got it. Never heard of it. Thanks. Pardon the interruption, please continue.
[2019-10-17 23:57] hyreon: We made up the term today, so that makes sense
[2019-10-17 23:57] jeacaveo (jeacaveo (#NeverBrowserer)): 🤦 so I was the first victim.
[2019-10-17 23:58] hyreon: AO4 is what I call Punf's system, where you have (P1 and P3) vs (P2 and P4), with 6/6/6/7 econ. It's good, but you can't do simultaneous turns, and it gets unbalanced faster. Still more fair than 6/7 in base set, so still great. But not as good as CO4. (It's Alternating Order 4-player)
[2019-10-17 23:59] vargus2254870 (Vargus225): CO4 runs into p1 determining too much, am I right?
[2019-10-18 00:01] hyreon: No, they only have one turn, and they're probably going to just do DD. Then p2 and p3 get the first *simultaneous* turn.
[2019-10-18 00:03] hyreon: Come to think of it, we could do Simultaneous Order 4-player (SO4): (P1 and P2) vs (P3 and P4), with 6/7/7/8. It would function exactly the same in almost every game, but without actually splitting T1's turn for the first move.
